content.problemStatement:
After successfully defending her title of World Chess Champion, Ada noticed that she is lacking in rook endgames.

To improve her endgame skills, Ada placed KKK kings on a chessboard with NNN rows and MMM columns, and now she is calculating the minimum number of rooks she must place on the board to satisfy the following conditions:


No king is attacked by a rook.
The kings are isolated, i.e. no king can reach another king without passing through a square attacked by a rook or capturing a rook.
Help Ada check her answers.
constraints: each string contains only characters '.' and 'K'
the sum of N⋅MN \cdot MN⋅M over all test cases does not exceed 1,0241,0241,024
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The next NNN lines describe the chessboard. Each of these lines contains a string with length MMM describing one row, where an empty square and a square containing a king are denoted by '.' and 'K' respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum required number of rooks, or −1-1−1 if it is impossible to place rooks in a way that satisfies all conditions.
